criteria_verification.criterion_text: `record_stage_event()` is the single helper every stage hook calls
criteria_verification.evidence: Added record_stage_event in src/forge/memory/ordering.py and re-exported it from forge.memory (added to __all__). TestRecordStageEventIsSingleHelper asserts the helper is callable, lives in forge.memory.ordering, is reachable as `from forge.memory import record_stage_event`, and is listed in ordering_module.__all__.
criteria_verification.notes: Added record_stage_event in src/forge/memory/ordering.py and re-exported it from forge.memory (added to __all__). TestRecordStageEventIsSingleHelper asserts the helper is callable, lives in forge.memory.ordering, is reachable as `from forge.memory import record_stage_event`, and is listed in ordering_module.__all__.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: SQLite commit happens BEFORE Graphiti dispatch (no parallel; no reordering)
criteria_verification.evidence: record_stage_event calls persist_to_sqlite() synchronously and only after it returns invokes fire_and_forget_write(entity, group_id). TestRecordStageEventOrdering uses a shared call-order list to prove `sqlite` is appended before `graphiti`, and additionally asserts the entity returned by SQLite is the exact value forwarded to the Graphiti dispatcher.
criteria_verification.notes: record_stage_event calls persist_to_sqlite() synchronously and only after it returns invokes fire_and_forget_write(entity, group_id). TestRecordStageEventOrdering uses a shared call-order list to prove `sqlite` is appended before `graphiti`, and additionally asserts the entity returned by SQLite is the exact value forwarded to the Graphiti dispatcher.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: If SQLite commit raises, Graphiti dispatch does NOT happen (no orphan
criteria_verification.evidence: The implementation calls fire_and_forget_write only after persist_to_sqlite() returns, so any exception raised by the callable propagates and short-circuits dispatch. TestRecordStageEventSqliteFailure patches fire_and_forget_write and asserts spy.assert_not_called() across three failure shapes (custom subclass of RuntimeError, ValueError, and a sentinel RuntimeError instance), and verifies the original exception is propagated verbatim.
criteria_verification.notes: The implementation calls fire_and_forget_write only after persist_to_sqlite() returns, so any exception raised by the callable propagates and short-circuits dispatch. TestRecordStageEventSqliteFailure patches fire_and_forget_write and asserts spy.assert_not_called() across three failure shapes (custom subclass of RuntimeError, ValueError, and a sentinel RuntimeError instance), and verifies the original exception is propagated verbatim.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: If Graphiti dispatch fails, SQLite entry is still durable (verified by
criteria_verification.evidence: record_stage_event returns the SQLite-persisted entity regardless of Graphiti dispatch outcome — fire_and_forget_write is contractually silent-swallow, so the helper never undoes the SQLite commit. TestRecordStageEventGraphitiFailure exercises the realistic quiet-dispatch path (returns the entity for reconcile-backfill to pick up) and confirms the helper does not block on dispatcher completion. Reconcile-backfill (TASK-IC-004) will detect SQLite-anchored entries missing from Graphiti.
criteria_verification.notes: record_stage_event returns the SQLite-persisted entity regardless of Graphiti dispatch outcome — fire_and_forget_write is contractually silent-swallow, so the helper never undoes the SQLite commit. TestRecordStageEventGraphitiFailure exercises the realistic quiet-dispatch path (returns the entity for reconcile-backfill to pick up) and confirms the helper does not block on dispatcher completion. Reconcile-backfill (TASK-IC-004) will detect SQLite-anchored entries missing from Graphiti.

criteria_verification.evidence: TestRecordStageEventTimestampPrecedence captures a wall-clock timestamp inside the patched fire_and_forget_write at dispatch time and asserts the entity's SQLite-side decided_at is strictly less than that captured value. A second test flips a `persist_returned` marker just before the persist callable returns and asserts the dispatcher sees the marker as True — proving dispatch only runs after persist returns. The BDD step impl for `@edge-case write-ordering` is the responsibility of TASK-IC-011 per the task spec.
criteria_verification.notes: TestRecordStageEventTimestampPrecedence captures a wall-clock timestamp inside the patched fire_and_forget_write at dispatch time and asserts the entity's SQLite-side decided_at is strictly less than that captured value. A second test flips a `persist_returned` marker just before the persist callable returns and asserts the dispatcher sees the marker as True — proving dispatch only runs after persist returns. The BDD step impl for `@edge-case write-ordering` is the responsibility of TASK-IC-011 per the task spec.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
criteria_verification.evidence: Ran `black --check src/forge/memory/ordering.py src/forge/memory/__init__.py tests/unit/test_ordering.py` — all three files report `would be left unchanged`. Both files use 4-space indentation, 88-char max line length, snake_case functions, type hints throughout, and follow the existing module-docstring conventions in src/forge/memory/.
criteria_verification.notes: Ran `black --check src/forge/memory/ordering.py src/forge/memory/__init__.py tests/unit/test_ordering.py` — all three files report `would be left unchanged`. Both files use 4-space indentation, 88-char max line length, snake_case functions, type hints throughout, and follow the existing module-docstring conventions in src/forge/memory/.
